  • Southern Africa

Southern Africa

Southern Africa combines polished infrastructure, big safari names, desert routes, and city-and-bush pairings.

Travelers can mix luxury lodges, self-drive landscapes, waterfalls, wine, and long scenic road circuits without losing practical route logic.

Regional overview

Southern Africa is one of the continent’s most versatile travel regions. South Africa acts as the gateway, Botswana and Namibia deliver high-value wilderness and desert landscapes, and Zimbabwe and Zambia deepen the safari-and-Zambezi story.

These countries are the strongest booking and route-building anchors across Southern Africa.

Use this page to

  • Compare the countries in Southern Africa that are most suitable for visitors.
  • Open a country landing page for practical travel context.
  • Jump onward into attractions, stays, and restaurants from the right country base.

Countries

Countries in Southern Africa

Every country links to its own landing page with a general overview, featured attractions, tour operators, stays, and restaurants.

Southern Africa

South Africa

South Africa is the region’s most varied travel gateway, offering city design, wine, beaches, and major safari access within one country.

Country guide
View Details

Southern Africa

Botswana

Botswana is a high-value safari market built around the Okavango Delta, Chobe, and low-impact conservation-led travel.

Country guide
View Details

Southern Africa

Namibia

Namibia is one of Africa’s strongest road-trip countries, defined by desert scale, low-density landscapes, and exceptional scenery.

Country guide
View Details

Southern Africa

Zimbabwe

Zimbabwe delivers Victoria Falls, Hwange, and some of Africa’s strongest guiding traditions in a more lightly crowded setting.

Country guide
View Details

Southern Africa

Zambia

Zambia is revered for walking safaris, strong wildlife density, and a rugged sense of authenticity on the Zambezi and Luangwa systems.

Country guide
View Details

Featured attractions

High-interest attractions in Southern Africa

South Africa

Cape Town

A design-led city with ocean views, wine access, and easy day trips.

★ 4.8 (1,675) From $20
Western Cape
View Details

Zimbabwe

Victoria Falls

The classic waterfall gateway for rainforest walks, river activities, and onward safari routing.

★ 4.7 (882) From $28
Zimbabwe
View Details

South Africa

Kruger National Park

A major Big Five safari zone with wide lodge choice and strong self-drive or guided options.

★ 4.8 (913) From $85
Mpumalanga and Limpopo
View Details

Botswana

Okavango Delta

One of Africa’s most exclusive safari ecosystems with mokoro channels and floodplain camps.

★ 4.9 (341) From $550
Northern Botswana
View Details

Namibia

Namib Desert

Towering dunes, cinematic desert light, and an unmatched sense of open space.

★ 4.8 (402) From $65
Sossusvlei and Namib-Naukluft
View Details

Zambia

South Luangwa National Park

One of Africa’s strongest walking safari landscapes.

★ 4.8 (273) From $120
Eastern Zambia
View Details
